questions.text: Which Prophet's story is the most prominent in Surah An-Naml?
questions.answer: Prophet Sulayman (Solomon).

questions.text: What specific blessing did Allah give Dawud and Sulayman that they were grateful for?
questions.answer: Knowledge, specifically the language of birds (Mantiq al-Tayr).

questions.text: What did the female ant say when she saw Sulayman's army approaching?
questions.answer: 'O ants, enter your dwellings lest Sulayman and his hosts crush you while they perceive not'.

questions.text: What was Sulayman's (AS) reaction to the ant's speech?
questions.answer: He smiled, laughing at her speech, and prayed to Allah to enable him to be grateful for His favors.

questions.text: Which bird was missing from Sulayman's assembly?
questions.answer: The Hoopoe (Hud-hud).

questions.text: What news did the Hoopoe bring from Saba (Sheba)?
questions.answer: He found a woman (Bilqis) ruling over them with a magnificent throne, but she and her people were worshipping the sun instead of Allah.

questions.text: How did Sulayman (AS) respond to the gift of gold and wealth sent by Bilqis?
questions.answer: He rejected it, stating that what Allah had given him was better than what He had given them.

questions.text: Who brought the throne of Bilqis to Sulayman in the 'twinkling of an eye'?
questions.answer: One who had knowledge of the Scripture.

questions.text: Why did Bilqis think the floor of Sulayman's palace was water?
questions.answer: Because it was made of smooth, clear glass over flowing water.

questions.text: What was Bilqis's final realization after entering the palace?
questions.answer: She realized she had wronged herself and submitted with Sulayman to Allah, Lord of the worlds.

questions.text: What did the nine men in the city of Prophet Salih plot to do?
questions.answer: They took a mutual oath to attack Salih and his family by night and then lie to his protector.

questions.text: What was the response of Prophet Lut's people to his call (Verse 56)?
questions.answer: They said 'Expel the family of Lut from your city; indeed, they are people who keep themselves pure'.

questions.text: Which of Lut's (AS) family members was not saved?
questions.answer: His wife; she was decreed to be among those who remained behind for punishment.

questions.text: How many times does the Surah ask 'Is there a god with Allah?' (A-ilahun ma'allah)?
questions.answer: Five times, following descriptions of Allah's creative power and provision.

questions.text: Who responds to the distressed when he calls upon Him?
questions.answer: Allah alone (Verse 62).

questions.text: What is the 'Dabbat al-Ard' (Beast of the Earth) mentioned in Verse 82?
questions.answer: A sign of the Day of Judgment that will emerge and speak to people because they did not have certainty in Allah's signs.

questions.text: What will happen when the Trumpet is blown (Verse 87)?
questions.answer: Everyone in the heavens and earth will be terrified, except whom Allah wills, and all will come to Him in humility.

questions.text: How are the mountains described in their movement on the Last Day?
questions.answer: They appear firm, but they will pass away like the passing of clouds.

questions.text: What is the reward for one who brings a good deed (Hasanah)?
questions.answer: He will have better than it, and they will be safe from the terror of that Day.

questions.text: What was the Prophet (SAW) commanded to be among (Verse 91)?
questions.answer: Among the Muslims (those who submit to Allah).
